prompt:
The gym-stats crate stopped building on this branch and the errors span three unrelated-looking corners of src/lib.rs. Full headline list:

  error[E0117]: only traits defined in the current crate can be implemented for types defined outside of the crate
    --> src/lib.rs:78:1   (impl fmt::Display for Vec<Ascent>)
  error[E0308]: `if` and `else` have incompatible types
    --> src/lib.rs:72:9   — expected `Map<Iter<'_, Ascent>, {closure@lib.rs:69}>`, found a different closure
  error[E0277]: the trait bound `T: Clone` is not satisfied
    --> src/lib.rs:47:35  — entries.to_vec() in strongest_half
  error[E0277]: `T` doesn't implement `Debug`
    --> src/lib.rs:57:67  — the zero-score trace line in rank
  error: could not compile `rs-cragstats` (lib) due to 4 previous errors

Fair warning from my own poking: every time I patched one of the generic ones, the same complaint popped up again one function further out, so budget for that. Constraints from the team: the summary stack MUST stay generic over Scored — the boulder-league importer feeds it its own row type, and the tests hold us to that — and every rendered string (meeting summary, tag lines, wall card) is pinned exactly by tests/cragstats.rs. cargo test --offline green, without touching tests/, Cargo.toml, or .gitignore.
notes: Trait-bound triage: missing Clone (to_vec in strongest_half) and Debug (trace line in rank) bounds that must propagate up the 3-level generic stack rank -> strongest_half -> meeting_summary; an impl-Trait return with two different closure types in the if/else arms (E0308); and an orphan-rule violation (Display for Vec<Ascent>, E0117) whose reference fix is a private SetCard newtype — the tests only pin wall_card's output string, not the mechanism. Chain-reveal, documented per 5C law and flagged in the user prompt: rustc 1.96.0 reports the E0277s at the innermost use first; adding the bound there re-raises the same E0277 at each caller in turn (2 further rounds). The league-importer test defines its own Scored type so de-genericizing the stack to Ascent fails.
